name: First State Bank Of Swanville
description_short: First State Bank of Swanville is a locally owned community bank serving Todd and Morrison Counties since 1907, offering personal and business banking services.
description_long: First State Bank of Swanville has operated as a community-focused financial institution for over 115 years, establishing itself as a fixture in the Swanville, Minnesota area and surrounding counties. The bank emphasizes its identity as a small-town operation with personalized customer service, positioning itself as an alternative to larger regional or national banking chains. The institution is independently owned and operated locally rather than being part of a larger banking network. Founded in 1907, the bank has maintained continuous operations throughout significant economic periods, suggesting established stability and local community integration. The bank offers a full suite of consumer and small business banking products. Personal banking services include checking and savings accounts, online banking, and mobile banking through a free downloadable app. Business banking services are available with corresponding loan options for small business owners. The bank provides mobile banking functionality allowing customers to check balances, transfer money, and manage accounts remotely using existing Internet Banking credentials. All digital services are positioned as free to customers. What distinguishes First State Bank of Swanville is its hyper-local presence and emphasis on personalized service rather than technological innovation or extensive product breadth. The bank operates a single main office location in Swanville with three strategically placed ATMs at local businesses (Lucky's Saloon, The Hub Supper Club, and Johnson's Market) rather than a traditional ATM network. Customer contact is facilitated through direct phone, fax, and email channels with staff available during posted lobby and drive-through hours. The bank explicitly markets itself as offering "small town individual customer service" and "solutions to banking needs" rather than comprehensive financial product ecosoning. The honest assessment is that First State Bank of Swanville is a legitimate community bank suitable for customers prioritizing local relationships and straightforward banking services. However, the single-location footprint, limited ATM network, and restricted hours (lobby closed Saturdays and Sundays, limited Friday evening access) may inconvenience customers requiring extended accessibility. The bank does not advertise premium products like investment services, wealth management, or specialized lending programs beyond standard personal and business accounts.
pros: Established 115+ year history (since 1907) indicating stability and community integration
Free mobile banking app with balance checking and money transfer capabilities
Three strategically placed ATM locations at local businesses for community access
Direct personal contact channels (phone, fax, email) with named staff
No mention of monthly account fees, suggesting straightforward account structures
Business banking and loan options available alongside personal accounts
cons: Single physical branch location limits accessibility for customers outside immediate Swanville area
Lobby hours limited to 3:00pm-4:30pm weekdays with no Sunday access, restricting in-person banking
Only three ATM locations, all within local Swanville/Burtrum area, no broader ATM network
Website lacks information on account fees, interest rates, or specific loan terms
No mention of expanded digital features like bill pay, peer-to-peer transfers, or account aggregation
best_for: Swanville and Todd/Morrison County residents prioritizing local banking relationships
Small business owners in the local area seeking community bank financing and business accounts
Customers seeking straightforward checking/savings without complex products
Individuals wanting direct personal contact with banking staff
diagnosis: First State Bank of Swanville is best for residents and small business owners in the Swanville/Todd-Morrison County area who value direct personal relationships with banking staff and straightforward account products. The primary caveat is the single-location footprint and limited operating hours, which make this impractical for customers requiring broader accessibility or living outside the immediate service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
